NAME: Orchard Gardens K-8 School
Program Description::
We partner with you to:
 -Identify and group students with a shared learning need
 -Develop high-quality, standards-aligned instruction from Imagine Learning’s core content
 -Deliver targeted interventions using research-based best practices
 -Use formal and formative assessments to measure growth and inform instruction
 -Support multilingual learners with ESOL-endorsed instructors
 -Provide comprehensive and ongoing reporting
 -Meet students where they are to drive growth

Instruction delivered within a 12-week cycle; each group will meet for three (3) 30-minute sessions per week. Please note: the number of weeks, sessions per week, and length of sessions is flexible.

Choose from a variety of instructional models — each led by a certified teacher:

INTERVENTION WITHIN THE SCHOOL DAY - Certified teachers seamlessly deliver push-in or pull-out online sessions during intervention time to students who need support to work up to grade level.

IN-CLASS STATION ROTATION - Support your educators by providing additional staff to deliver station-based, small-group support in the classroom.

COMPUTER LAB ROTATION - Make the most of your computer lab time by providing students with live, high-quality instruction.

BEFORE- OR AFTER-SCHOOL SUPPORT - Support students with extra practice before or after school with high-dosage tutoring.

SUMMER SCHOOL -Take a proactive approach to help students reach grade-level success by providing small-group support over the summer.

NAME: Blackstone Elementary School
Program Description:: Mindfulness is a proven intervention that reduces stress and anxiety, which are at the root of most mental health disorders. The audio-guided series of 5-10-min/day practices are based on the world-renowned Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) protocol. The program requires no teacher training and is easy to fit into PK-12 curriculums. Teachers’ login and press play and practice with their classes so they too, realize the benefits of these breathing and relaxation exercises. This is a Tier 1 mental health intervention being used in 7000 schools and available to all kids, teachers, and their families. Studies show daily mindfulness practices reduces stress by 43%, decreases behavioral events by 60% and increases grades by 9-15%. Inner Explorer is approved by CASEL and recommended by Brain Futures and the USDOE. Inner Explorer qualifies for ESSER funding under numerous categories. The Shah Family Foundation is currently funding an Inner Explorer pilot in 10 schools.

NAME: Hennigan Elementary School
Program Description:: The purpose of the ALP is to provide an achievement-oriented, participatory educational program to educationally disadvantaged and Limited English Proficient adults. Our goal is to help students attain the education, skills, and self-confidence that will enable them to more fully participate in their communities and in the education of their children, and that will further enhance opportunities for personal development and economic self-sufficiency. To this end, we provide Teachers, Support Staff, materials and a supportive environment in which students can build upon their life experiences to improve their English speaking, reading, writing, math, and career readiness skills.

NAME: Higginson/Lewis K-8 School
Program Description::
KeySteps mission is to provide a comprehensive range of wrap around services and support for at-risk school-age youth in order to ensure that they stay in school and gain the skills and emotional resiliency to beome educated productive adults and citizens who contribute to society.
In 2012, we changed our name from CSAPP to KeySteps to reflect the services provided & model of comprehensive services developed to prevent dropout & promote completion of school. The KeySteps model engages youth of many different cultures, their parents, school-based staff & community based personnel to help maximize teachers' ability to teach & students' capacity to succeed.
Because our Masters level, licensed counselors embedded in BPS high schools & middle schools have met with outstanding success, KeySteps has earned the trust of our youth, their parents, educators, collaborators & funders.
Comprehensive counseling, clinical case management, information & referral services are offered year round
and KeySteps summertime programming includes experiential learning activities, exposure to opportunity & ongoing outreach & home visitation for youth who are homebound.
Because services are full time, year round & co-located in our schools, access to youth prior to dropout is heightened; opportunity to mediate fast flexible support tailored to the needs of each youth is increased & the ability to create high expectations & long-term trusting relationships, promoted.
Explanation of Student Fees: no fees

NAME: Blackstone Elementary School
Program Description::
A recent study by EdResearch for Recovery suggests that high-dosage in-school tutoring may be one of the best strategies for addressing the educational impact of COVID-19 (Accelerating Student Learning, 2021). Our literacy coaches receive on average 25 hours of training throughout the year that teaches skills in working with early readers, implementing our session structure, working with English language learners, cultural responsiveness training, and more. Literations literacy coaches serve an average of 4 years- more than twice the industry average. 

Our program in Boston Public Schools is supported by two Program Managers, Literations staff members who oversee program implementation at partner sites. Literacy sessions take place during normal school hours and align with the school year. As school day structures vary across partner schools, Program Managers coordinate with school administration and faculty to develop a program calendar that integrates our program into each school’s unique daily and weekly schedule. Once a program calendar is established, school partners are assigned a Site Team Leader (STL), an experienced literacy coach who has received additional training in leadership and program management, to coordinate the day-to-day operations of the Sustained Tutoring Program. STLs are responsible for coordinating literacy session schedules with teachers and providing on-site supervision and quality control for volunteers and school faculty.

NAME: Trotter Elementary School
Program Description:: The Reading Coaches Program is a structured literacy tutoring program for students in grades K2-3, which provides 1:1 tutoring twice a week using American Reading Company curriculum. It is a great program for young readers who need extra help and we get strong outcomes. Teachers refer students to this program, which is a "pull-out" program.  The coaches are professionally trained older adults from the community and are highly committed to increasing the literacy proficiency skills of young students.

NAME: Channing Elementary School
Organization Name:: Little Voices After School @ The Channing
Program Description::
The purpose of Little Voices Early Care and Education is to provide high quality education and childcare for children between the ages of zero to six years old and their families in and around the greater Boston area. 

We also offer an after school program for children in elementary schools in the area. This program is devoted to providing quality childcare at an affordable price for parents.Little Voices Offers afterschhol programming and out of school time care for students. UPK is also offered at nearby programs.

NAME: Ellis Elementary School
Program Description::
LiveSchool simplifies schoolwide behavior management by giving educators tools to consistently recognize and reinforce positive behavior. With just a few taps, teachers can award points aligned with the school’s expectations, helping students see real-time progress toward goals.

Beyond tracking points, LiveSchool delivers actionable data to help leaders understand trends, celebrate wins, and address challenges proactively. Administrators and educators can analyze engagement patterns, identify areas of improvement, and ensure recognition is equitable and meaningful across classrooms.

Students and families receive weekly LiveSchool Recaps, keeping everyone informed and connected. The platform’s transparency builds accountability and trust—students know what’s expected, and parents can easily follow along.

Ultimately, LiveSchool is about more than rewarding good behavior. It’s a system for shaping school culture, supporting social-emotional learning, and empowering every student to thrive in a positive, inclusive environment.

NAME: Kilmer K-8 School
Program Description:: Making Music Matters is a collaborative school based partnership that gives students group instrumental lessons in clarinet, flute, trumpet, and violin during the regular school day. Our youngest eligible students are taught an introductory class and learn all four instruments. Students in the upper grades will pick one instrument to continue learning. All students will have the opportunity to perform in school concerts and other community events to be determined.
Explanation of Student Fees: Making Music Matters charges a suggested fee of $50 to rent an instrument for the school year. These fees help to pay for rentals, supplies and upkeep for all instruments. We do offer financial assistance to families that require it.

NAME: Boston Teachers Union Elementary Pilot School
Program Description::
We’d like to offer in-person small group music lessons in your after-school care program. 
We offer beginner guitar and piano “clubs.”
We prefer to group them by grade-groups: K-2 children in one lesson group, 3-5 in another lesson group. 
With our methods, children begin by finding the notes on the keyboard or the names of the strings and the numbers of the frets rather than reading music right away. We want them to have fun and success with even their first lesson, so we make sure they can play a familiar tune, such as “Mary Had a Little Lamb” or “Are You Sleeping Brother John,” by the end of their first lesson.
Our teachers are educated professional musicians who have been fully vetted and background checked by GoodHire, and will go through BPS CORI once we have a site at which they will be working. 
We organize recitals at the end of each 8 to 10-week session and invite the parents to come.
Explanation of Student Fees: Our normal charge is $24.75 per student per lesson. Our sister organization, The Village Music School Foundation, can subsidize up to 90% of the fee for one Title I child per class. If there are ten children in the class, The VMSF can subsidize 2 of them.

NAME: Mattahunt Elementary School
Program Description:: musiConnects mission is to model and teach self-expression, peer leadership and community development through the transformative power of chamber music. Founded in 2007, musiConnects is a performance and education organization and is home to nine professional Resident Musicians who teach and perform. All enrolled students receive private one on one instruction on the violin, viola or cello and are placed in a chamber music group. We also offer orchestra, music theory and other music courses depending on the age and level of student. We work with students from K to 12, preferring to create long-term relationships and build deep ties in one community.
Explanation of Student Fees: All programming is provided free of charge including high quality instrument rentals and materials.

NAME: Hurley K-8 School
Program Description::
One Bead’s Pathways Program enhances the educational experience for youth in Boston by bringing diverse leaders into classrooms to share their career journeys.

When we partner with a school, we typically serve an entire grade level, ensuring that every child has access to our programming over time. Once a school partnership is established, One Bead instructors meet with teachers to review curriculum goals and objectives. From there, instructors identify guest speakers in the community who can effectively translate these goals into engaging learning experiences. Each session is structured as an interactive workshop with a youth-led Q&A followed by a leadership skill-building activity linked to the speaker’s role and/or industry.

In addition to running the Pathways Program in classrooms, One Bead instructors also support school leaders by identifying and prepping guest speakers for assemblies and other school-wide events, and coordinating field trips to local work spaces. 

By the end of the school year, our goal is to expose students to a variety of career paths and leaders, ensuring that every child, regardless of background, feels empowered to achieve success in school and beyond.

NAME: Higginson/Lewis K-8 School
Program Description::
One on One Learning provides targeted academic intervention and tutoring services designed to accelerate student achievement through individualized, data-driven instruction. Our program is built on a proven model that identifies learning gaps, addresses students’ unique needs, and delivers instruction aligned with state standards and district goals.
Using educators and trained interventionists, we deliver services before, during, and after school in small group or one-on-one settings. Every session is tailored to support student growth in core academic areas such as reading, math, writing, and English language development.
We collaborate closely with school leaders and teachers to design a program, align instruction, and ensure measurable progress. In addition to accelerating learning, our approach emphasizes relationship-building, academic confidence, and consistent feedback to support the whole child. Progress monitoring is embedded throughout the tutoring program to measure gains and guide instruction.

Our team ensures students make meaningful gains. The result is a responsive, flexible solution that helps students thrive—academically, socially, and emotionally.

With over 25 years of experience and a deep commitment to academic gains, One on One Learning is a trusted partner for schools seeking to close achievement gaps and increase student success.

NAME: Boston Teachers Union Elementary Pilot School
Program Description::
OrigiNation Dance! will be taught by our Artistic Director, a professionally trained instructor possessing a wide range of experiences, and dance disciplines. OrigiNation Dance! will explore Hip Hop and African Dance although she teaches over seven genres. Our leadership commits to ensuring that every student’s voice is heard and their identity respected. We prioritize the student experience of joy, and diversity the content we teach, highlighting global dance traditions plus contemporary works from various creators.
OrigiNation aims to bring fresh perspective and additional expertise, filling gaps in school programming. (IE: we train dance forms that remain under-represented, and our work enhances the skill set of existing staff.) We engage families, work with community organizations, and have developed showcase spaces that connect student talent to local community. Our involvement can help schools strengthen local partnerships, broadening student experience and exposure to professional dance networks.
Possible curriculum: emotional expression in dance, cultural origins and social justice influences, choreography, freestyle and leadership opportunities.
In the past, our recruitment efforts have successfully increased student participation in the OrigiNation spring, summer and year-round programs. After exposure to OrigiNation, student retention and interest in dance remains strong, enhancing student leadership development, academic accountability, and healthy engagement.
Explanation of Student Fees: The register fee is $800.00. The fee includes free dance attire, costumes for participants, a summer program t-shirt. and admission to all field trips.

NAME: Madison Park Technical Vocational High School
School Name:: Madison Park Technical Vocational High School
Program Description:: PYD’s Career Readiness Program is an inclusive job-readiness program that addresses barriers to employment for youth and young adults with disabilities. This program has existed for over 25 years. Our Career Readiness Program addresses barriers to employment via a three-tiered curriculum involving academic learning, real-world experiences, and mentoring. The curriculum is based upon the principles of Universal Design for Learning and includes flexible goals, methods, materials, assessments, and accommodations for learning differences.
Program Subareas:: 'Career Advising','Job & Employment Preparation','Mentoring','Vocation Placement','Work-based Learning','Workforce Development'

NAME: King Elementary School
Program Description::
Playworks coach focuses on implementing four key areas:

1. Recess - The coach creates a safe, fun, and inclusive playground by developing and modelling standard agreements for behavior and conflict resolution skills, teaching kids the rules and strategies of the games, and recruiting disengaged students to participate.

2. Junior Coach Leadership Program - Junior Coaches are a mix of 4th through 6th grade students who participate in regular trainings that focus on teaching valuable skills like leadership, conflict resolution, inclusion and community action, successfully improving their self-esteem, self advocacy, leadership ability, and desire to improve academically.

3. Class Game Time - The coach leads instructional time with individual classes throughout the year. During this time, the coach teaches foundational skills and activities that will prepare students so they can confidently engage in and enjoy recess.

4. Interscholastic Leagues - The coach leads non-competitive teams designed to develop skills, provide a positive team experience, and teach good sporting behavior to students.
Time: School Day (Class Time), Afterschool, School Day (Non-Class Time)
Explanation of Student Fees: There is a school fee of $31,000.00 due per year (not charged to students).

NAME: Haynes Early Education Center
Program Description::
It’s Bigger Than Basketball
From the beginning, our mission has been to develop our athletes into the best versions of themselves using basketball as a vehicle of teaching. Our approach revolves around our Big 3: Fundamentals, Basketball IQ and Personal Development. We are committed to guiding our students towards excellence both in their athletic pursuits and personal growth, fostering a culture of healthy competition and learning within a supportive environment led by dedicated trainers and mentors. We aim to serve over 10 cities throughout the Greater Boston and Middlesex County areas, positively impacting the lives of over 2,500 young athletes. We consider it a privilege to help athletes of all skill levels provided they demonstrate consistency, hard-work and dedication. Our expectations are that our athletes exhibit constant growth, no matter how big or small their goals. PTW places a strong emphasis on leaving a lasting mark on our athletes, transcending their time with our program. The world today presents many challenges for the youth. All young individuals deserve a chance to become the best version of themselves both mentally and physically. At PTW, we chose success the right way – It’s Bigger than Basketball.

NAME: Roosevelt K-8 School
Program Description:: Raising A Reader MA is an evidence-based early literacy program that helps families of young children (birth through age six) develop, practice, and maintain habits of reading together at home. Our core program model, which both increases access to books and offers support for strengthening the culture of reading at home, is driven by more than 25 years of research that shows a significant factor impacting a child’s academic success is being regularly read to by a parent or other primary adult caregiver before starting kindergarten.

NAME: Adams Elementary School
Program Name:: Read to a Child Read Aloud Mentoring Program
Program Description:: Volunteer mentors, usually from nearby partner corporations and some from the Community, read aloud 1:1 to students in person at the school or virtually via Zoom. Our Program Coordinator will facilitate the read aloud sessions in the school or she  will host the sessions on Zoom and manage contacts and logistics with the assistance of teachers and school personnel. Students in grades K-4 are referred by their teachers for the program. This will include contact with teachers directly to set up schedules, possibly change schedules, obtain teacher referrals for the program, or consult about a student in the program, and gather student assessments at the end of the program year. The program will run from from October to end of May/early June.
Explanation of Student Fees: Read to a Child covers all costs associated with the program.

NAME: Hennigan Elementary School
Program Description::
Sandy Spin Slade, Inc., DBA Skillastics® has been a cornerstone in the world of after-school programming and physical education for over 23 years.  Our innovative approach to simplifying physical activity is currently used in over 25,000 after-school programs, physical education classes, and community-based organizations nationwide. 
From the start, we set out to create a user-friendly curriculum that removed the common physical activity barriers faced by after-school program leaders and educators. Skillastics® makes physical activity sessions easy to implement through step-by-step lesson plans, reducing staff stress and frustration while ensuring lots of fun for kids.  

Skillastics® is extremely versatile, offering a range of activities that focus on fitness, sport skill development, social-emotional learning (SEL), and character education.  Each module is designed to be adaptable, whether sessions take place indoors or outdoors, with or without equipment, allowing for flexibility to meet the specific needs of different groups.
We pride ourselves in our commitment to continuous support and professional development.  We provide extensive online resources and ongoing training that ensure staff are well-equipped on delivering effective and engaging physical activity lessons.  
The blend of simplicity, variety, and long-term support has made Skillastics® a trusted resource in promoting physical & mental health, collaboration, and skill-building among children of all ages.

NAME: Quincy Elementary School
Program Description:: Our core program is the In-School Module-Based (ISMB) Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math (STEM) enrichment program. In this program, SciSci partners with elementary and middle schools that serve students in our target population: grades 2-8. In-person, SciSci sends the same two real, charismatic scientists into each of these Partner schools to see the same group of students every other week throughout the entire school year. Topics include chemistry, physics, biology, computer technology, engineering design, and more. All lessons are written in the 5E Instructional Model for inquiry-based learning and are informed by the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS), the national K-12 Framework for Science Education, and the Massachusetts Science and Technology/Engineering (STE) Framework.
Explanation of Student Fees:
N/A
There is no cost to students. See description below for "Explanation of Fees"

NAME: Boston International High School & Newcomers Academy
Program Description::
Through cross-community collaboration, young people gain insights and connections that help them achieve their goals while building skills as bridge-building leaders. SparkShare believes that creating healthier communities requires leaders who can work across differences and understand how individual projects connect to broader systems of wellbeing. We utilize a human-centered design framework that teaches youth to listen deeply, understand different perspectives, and create solutions that
truly serve their communities.

We activate our network through:
-Three Annual Summits that bring together youth from diverse communities for transformative skill-building experiences in brainstorming, storytelling, and visioning

-Train-the-Trainer Programs that equip adult youth leaders with human-centered design
facilitation skills to support their teams year-round

-SparkShare Accelerator that provides direct weekly coaching to youth teams using our
design thinking toolkit to advance their community health initiatives

-Healthcare Partnerships that connect youth teams with health expertise and amplify their
impact on community wellbeing

NAME: Another Course To College
Program Description::
SCG will partner with your team to co-design a Strategy & Action Plan that strengthens employer engagement and expands opportunities for students. Our approach is grounded in empowerment: building on the work your district has already accomplished, clarifying next steps, and ensuring your team has the confidence and action plan to carry this forward. Together, we will focus on creating sustainable partnerships that connect directly to your programs, reduce barriers for students, and provide clear goals for measuring success.

This program includes three facilitated sessions with the Superintendent and/or High School Principal and up to 5 participants. Each session lasts approximately 60 minutes.

NAME: Blackstone Elementary School
Program Description::
SSYP’s innovative Parent Mentor Program (PMP) provides opportunities for parents to participate in their children’s education and to advocate for the Boston Public Schools system. Our PMP program is modeled on the highly successful Palenque Logan Square Neighborhood Association, which has been training Parent Mentors in Chicago for more than 25 years. 

Parent Mentors work 2.5 hours a day supporting student learning in the classroom by leading small-group breakouts and working one-on-one with individual students. On Fridays, they meet to debrief the week and participate in leadership trainings on topics including Schools and Parents as Partners, Self-Esteem, Workplace Skills, Child Development, Community Organizing, and Women’s Health. Whenever possible, outside trainers (from organizations like Families First) are brought in to provide specialized knowledge. Parents set personal goals such as finding a job, learning to speak English or finishing their high school diploma and receive coaching to meet those goals. 

An important element of the PMP is paying parent mentors an hourly wage for the hours they spend in the classrooms working with students and the training time. Providing paid positions -- above minimum wage, with raises in their second year -- is respectful of our participants’ time and skills and acknowledges the substantial contributions they make to their children’s schools. 

A full evaluation report is available.
Program Areas:: 'Academic Support & Enrichment','College, Career & Life Readiness','Family Engagement & Support'

On Fridays, they meet to debrief the week and participate in leadership trainings on topics including Schools and Parents as Partners, Self-Esteem, Workplace Skills, Child Development, Community Organizing, and Women’s Health. Whenever possible, outside trainers (from organizations like Families First) are brought in to provide specialized knowledge. Parents set personal goals such as finding a job, learning to speak English or finishing their high school diploma and receive coaching to meet those goals. 

An important element of the PMP is paying parent mentors an hourly wage for the hours they spend in the classrooms working with students and the training time. Providing paid positions -- above minimum wage, with raises in their second year -- is respectful of our participants’ time and skills and acknowledges the substantial contributions they make to their children’s schools. 

A full evaluation report is available.
Program Areas:: 'Academic Support & Enrichment','College, Career & Life Readiness','Family Engagement & Support'

NAME: Russell Elementary School
Program Name:: The Farm School Program for Visiting Schools
Program Description:: The Farm School is a non-profit organization with a mission of connecting people to the land. For over 35 years, our Program for Visiting Schools (PVS) welcomes over 1,500 children per year to help us care for our farm during 2.5-day overnight visits. Typically serving youth in grades 5 and up. The students come as a class, in groups of up to 40 children at one time to spend 2 and a half days full immersed in the farm. While they are here, the students find value in working on the farm and wake up to open landscape ready to care for our dairy cows, goats, horses, lambs, pigs, and chickens, then running over to help plant seeds, harvest crops, turn the soil, make fire wood, walk the trails of the forest and cook meals out of food harvested from the land together. The students and teachers see each other in new lights and create a feeling of community that persists when they return to their classrooms, and walk away experiencing the magic that can be found in nature as stewards of the earth. Outside of our school group trips we also offer individual week long summer overnight programs as well.
Explanation of Student Fees: Will be determined by the partner school.  Ranges from NO Fee to $434 based on the percentage of students that qualify for free/reduced lunch at the school.

NAME: Curley K-8 School
Program Description::
Nature Days connects public school children with the natural world, providing students in urban areas with meaningful outdoor experiences. 

A robust body of research shows that experiences in nature improve academic learning and personal development, as well as environmental stewardship. This is especially true for disadvantaged students and those who experience difficulties in conventional classrooms. 

With Nature Days, students spend one morning each week exploring the outdoors, guided by Think Outside’s nature-based educators and accompanied by BPS classroom teachers and family/caregiver volunteers.

The program model has five key elements:
1. Playful, curiosity-based learning: helps meet the needs of the whole child, including physical, social and emotional needs.
2. Inclusive programming: Students with special needs such as autism and ADHD often benefit substantially from this model.
3. Proximity to school: No busing needed; nature experiences are embedded in local green spaces in student communities.
4. Rain or shine: We provide appropriate gear for students to play and learn comfortably in cold or wet weather.
5. Extensive nature exposure: Students participate in 2.5 hour sessions each week for 10 weeks in the fall and 10 weeks in the spring.

Nature Days is based on the Forest School model adopted by thousands of trained practitioners around the world.

NAME: Murphy K-8 School
Program Description::
Since its founding  in 2009 with 16 Young and Old Soles, Sole Train: Boston Runs Together has grown to engage 300 youth at 20 sites, including many Boston Public Schools. The program is an evidence-based public health intervention focused on social, emotional and physical health. Sole Train offers students positive community and a chance to experience success and boost confidence and agency. As an embedded program within Boston Public Schools, ST is uniquely poised to promote meaningful community change by teaching personal wellness and the importance of positive community to young people who want strong community connections.
Sole Train youth, most of whom have little running experience, train with adult mentors over the course of a school year for Boston’s Run to Remember, which offers both a half marathon and a five mile race. Sole Train’s supportive community of caring adults and peers helps students overcome personal barriers—both psychological and fitness-related—to stretch themselves to achieve what can be a seemingly impossible goal. Sole Train youth learn to deconstruct the impossible in order to create success: they identify the intermediate steps that need to be taken to achieve big goals, attain the appropriate support to take those steps, and develop the self-confidence necessary to follow-through on their goals. Youth then learn to transfer these skills to other areas of their lives and gain valuable tools to reach their full potential.

NAME: Ruth Batson Academy
Program Description:: TBC @ RBA provides a team and corresponding resources to support a trauma-informed, restorative culture throughout the school. RBA is one of the largest middle schools in Boston Public Schools (BPS), serving primarily youth of color, with a high concentration of special education and English Language Learners. Trinity Boston entered the RBA (formerly McCormack) in 2013 to directly support a caseload of students with low attendance, at risk of dropping out of school, to help students feel engaged and successful. More broadly, we have created re-regulation spaces that provide activities to help students center themselves. The goal is to give students the safe space they need to promote a shift in the school’s culture and climate to impact all 600+ students.

NAME: Boston International High School & Newcomers Academy
Program Description::
Our coaching work focuses on every level of the school building. We offer coaching and workshops for 1) classroom teachers, 2) building coaches and literacy leads, and 3) building administration.  Each coaching contract is customized to the needs of the partner: this means that the number and frequency of coaching interactions varies accordingly.  

Before a partnership, we work with the school or youth-serving organization to conduct a needs assessment, create a work plan, and establish goals.

During a partnership, we engage in…
- 1:1 coaching that is flexible and responsive to youth and educator needs. Coaching activities include observations and debriefs, co-planning and looking at data and outcomes
- Professional development workshops focused on particular topics to grow educator’s skills and knowledge (examples: disciplinary literacy and student-centered academic conversations) 
- Facilitation of educator collaborative groups, such as grade level or subject teams 
- Thought partnership with school and organizational leadership, to plan and implement coordinated capacity building and professional learning for all staff

NAME: Mario Umana Academy
Program Name:: YMCA of Greater Boston @ the Umana School
Program Description::
The Summer Learning Academy at the Umana Academy is a 5-week program that blends morning academics in ELA and Math with afternoon enrichment activities. 
Academics take place each morning led by certified MA educators and paraprofessionals. Afternoons include hands on learning experiences that include topics in social studies, the arts, STEM, community learning and physical activity. Breakfast, lunch and snack are provided to the students daily.
What is unique about the Umana Summer Learning Academy is that we celebrate the diversity of language in this program and have 3-4 classrooms that deliver ELA instruction in Spanish each week.  For students enrolling in the program that have only been exposed to the English Language, we ensure there is at least once classroom where both ELA and Math are led in English.
Explanation of Student Fees: Sliding Scale

NAME: Albert D Holland School of Technology
School Name:: Albert D. Holland Highschool of Technology
Program Name:: YMCA of Greater Boston  Achievers Program
Program Description::
The Y Achievers Program is designed to provide the critical support that low income and first generation students need to complete post-secondary education. Annually, the Achievers programs serves roughly 200 high school students from Boston and surrounding communities. The Achievers program uses an evidence-based curriculum, which is based on five pillars: Academics, College Knowledge, Positive Relationships, Life Skills and Positive Identity. 
 
Our curriculum’s focus is on the holistic development of each of our students. Most college access programs concentrate solely on college knowledge and academic requirements, but our curriculum is unique because of it’s additional focus on “Positive Relationships,” “Life Skills” and “Positive Identity.” 

Our ability to influence these low income students in a variety of different settings throughout the year is critical to the program’s effectiveness. During the week, our students are supported, engaged and influenced at their local Y by a full time teen director. On the weekends, these students develop at our bi-weekly cohort meetings, which are intentionally located on the campus of Northeastern University to inspire hope and a belief that a college degree is in all of our student’s futures. These cohort meetings are taught by adult volunteers from community partner organizations such as State Street, Raytheon, Tufts Health Plan, Partners HealthCare, Eversource and Harvard Pilgrim.
Explanation of Student Fees: no fees

NAME: Gardner Pilot Academy
Program Name:: YMCA of Greater Boston Before and Afterschool Programs
Program Description:: When the school day ends, the learning and fun continue! Our YMCA Afterschool Programs provide a welcoming environment for school-age youth from dismissal until 6:00 p.m. each day. Designed to support the needs of elementary students, the program offers a balanced mix of learning, play, and exploration in a caring and structured setting. Led by trained, compassionate educators who understand the unique needs of young learners, each afternoon combines academic support, physical activity, and creative enrichment to help children develop essential skills while having fun with friends. Families can feel confident knowing their children are in a safe, licensed, and nurturing environment that fosters growth, confidence, and character. Each afternoon follows an engaging routine that supports the whole child—academically, socially, emotionally, and physically. Students begin their day with a healthy snack before transitioning into homework and quiet time for focused learning. Once assignments are complete, children participate in physical and structured activities such as games, outdoor play, and team challenges that promote coordination, teamwork, and healthy habits. The day concludes with enrichment and exploration through art, STEM, music, drama, or cultural projects. Whether led by staff or small student groups, these experiences inspire creativity, leadership, and collaboration—ensuring every child ends their day feeling connected, capable, and confident.
Explanation of Student Fees:
Program Fees and Financial Assistance
The YMCA Afterschool Program charges a weekly fee for participation in the before-school and/or after-school program. We understand that every family’s situation is unique and are committed to making our programs affordable and accessible to all. The YMCA accepts state childcare vouchers and can assist families in qualifying for financial assistance or dedicated YMCA funding slots. Our staff work closely with each family to help identify a weekly rate that is both manageable and sustainable, ensuring that every child has the opportunity to benefit from a safe, enriching after-school experience.

The YMCA accepts state childcare vouchers and can assist families in qualifying for financial assistance or dedicated YMCA funding slots. Our staff work closely with each family to help identify a weekly rate that is both manageable and sustainable, ensuring that every child has the opportunity to benefit from a safe, enriching after-school experience.

NAME: Mozart Elementary School
Program Name:: YMCA of Greater Boston Before and Afterschool Programs
Program Description:: When the school day ends, the learning and fun continue! Our YMCA Afterschool Programs provide a welcoming environment for school-age youth from dismissal until 6:00 p.m. each day. Designed to support the needs of elementary students, the program offers a balanced mix of learning, play, and exploration in a caring and structured setting. Led by trained, compassionate educators who understand the unique needs of young learners, each afternoon combines academic support, physical activity, and creative enrichment to help children develop essential skills while having fun with friends. Families can feel confident knowing their children are in a safe, licensed, and nurturing environment that fosters growth, confidence, and character. Each afternoon follows an engaging routine that supports the whole child—academically, socially, emotionally, and physically. Students begin their day with a healthy snack before transitioning into homework and quiet time for focused learning. Once assignments are complete, children participate in physical and structured activities such as games, outdoor play, and team challenges that promote coordination, teamwork, and healthy habits. The day concludes with enrichment and exploration through art, STEM, music, drama, or cultural projects. Whether led by staff or small student groups, these experiences inspire creativity, leadership, and collaboration—ensuring every child ends their day feeling connected, capable, and confident.

NAME: Boston Collaborative High School
Program Description::
City Ascent is an after school youth-belayed outdoor climbing program. YES brings the same group of 10 high school students for 5 trips to Hammond Pond (Newton) or Quincy Quarries (Qunicy). 

The aim of the program is to present physical and mental challenges to youth while helping to build trust, teamwork, and confidence through the sport of climbing.
Explanation of Student Fees: $40 per participant

NAME: Mildred Avenue K-8 School
Program Description::
BAM provides male students with the guidance and tools they need to develop into healthy, responsible young men and thrive. 

BAM offers students weekly group counseling sessions that are designed to develop social-emotional skills correlated with academic success and healthy behaviors. Each   group session, called a BAM Circle, helps students learn and practice specific skills through a blend of group activities, including role plays, storytelling, field trips, and group missions. 

Each skill taught through BAM aligns with six Core Values: Integrity, Accountability, Self-Determination, Positive Anger Expression, Respect for Womanhood, and Visionary Goal Setting.  

In addition to weekly BAM Circles, students will have access to individual mentoring and support from a BAM Counselor.

NAME: English High School
Program Description::
Working On Womanhood (WOW) is an in-school, group-based counseling and clinical mentoring program that cultivates leadership, promotes mental health, and fosters the social-emotional skills of girls and young women in grades 6th -12th. To do so, WOW applies a therapy-based approach that addresses gender-specific needs by increasing self-image and self-worth for female-identifying students. WOW strengthens critical protective factors and reduces high-risk behaviors among girls by helping them to cope with negative thoughts, interrogate their automatic responses, respond reflectively in high-stakes situations, solve problems, and build self-efficacy to make positive and healthy decisions, and ultimately, be leaders in their schools and their communities.

Through weekly sessions throughout the year, WOW participants engage in activities and discussions designed to improve self-image, self-worth and social skills. Each young woman benefits from interactions with her counselor and peers.

NAME: Curley K-8 School
Program Description::
The F.Y.R.E program is an empowerment and leadership program for middle school girls, inclusive of
trans and gender non-conforming students, within the Greater Boston area. It is an in-school and
after-school program that uses dialogue and experiential learning through a curriculum that focuses
on positive self-identity and social emotional skills, as well as social justice and civic engagement.

 

In our weekly program, participants gain the skills and knowledge to assess racial and gender inequities within Boston, build a sense of self and empowerment, and increase their capacity for engagement in advocacy as they examine their own needs and the needs of their community. At the end of the program, students will present a community action project at a F.Y.R.E. Initiative organized event where students have the opportunity to engage with other youth leaders across Greater Boston.
Program Subareas:: 'Cultural Proficiency','Identity Exploration','Social Emotional Learning','Student Leadership Development'
Explanation of Student Fees: A tiered system is in place for individuals based on family income. The fees range from $50-$1,000 for the ten month program. Sometimes, sponsoring schools opt to pay the individual fee on behalf of their students.
